content: MS diagnosis uses 2017 McDonald Criteria requiring dissemination in space (DIS) and time (DIT). DIS: ≥1 T2-hyperintense lesion in ≥2 of 4 CNS regions: periventricular, cortical/juxtacortical, infratentorial, spinal cord. DIT demonstrated by: (1) simultaneous presence of gadolinium-enhancing and non-enhancing lesions, OR (2) new T2 or enhancing lesion on follow-up MRI, OR (3) positive CSF oligoclonal bands (allows DIT diagnosis at first scan). MRI protocol: brain and entire spine with and without gadolinium. Typical findings: ovoid periventricular lesions perpendicular to ventricles (Dawson's fingers), juxtacortical U-fiber involvement, infratentorial lesions, short-segment spinal cord lesions (<3 vertebral segments). CSF: oligoclonal IgG bands present in >90% (not specific but supports diagnosis). Evoked potentials (visual, somatosensory, motor) detect subclinical pathway involvement. Differential diagnosis includes NMOSD (AQP4-IgG positive, longitudinally extensive transverse myelitis), MOGAD, vasculitis, sarcoidosis, B12 deficiency, Lyme disease.
